summary:
The March fracture involves the midshaft or diaphysis of the second metatarsal bone. This injury does not involve bony changes near a surgical site from procedures like bunionectomy. \boxed{Second Metatarsal Shaft}

The summary concisely captures that the March fracture primarily impacts the middle part of the second metatarsal without mentioning unrelated aspects such as surgeries for bunions. Thus providing clear information about which section of this specific foot bone it targets accurately addressing the query posed by focusing solely on clinically significant details pertinent to identifying affected anatomical regions during fractures specifically named "March".
